problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
mechanism: Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations
hypothesis: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Optogenetic. Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Randomized controlled trial Phase I with 33 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: What we observe is not nature itself but nature exposed to our method of questioning. — Heisenberg
